reasoning:
For the first resolution date (2026-02-08), the "FIDE rankings" Wikipedia page relies on the monthly Top 100 list published by FIDE. Given the cutoff is in early February, the page will almost certainly still display the 2026-02-01 standard list where Assaubayeva is rank 11. Baring vandalism or highly unusual corrections, this evaluates to YES. (95%, capped).

For the second resolution date (2026-03-03), Wikipedia will have updated to reflect the March 1, 2026 FIDE rankings. Assaubayeva's rating on Feb 1 is exactly tied with Divya Deshmukh at 2497 (she currently wins the tiebreak to be 11th). We established Assaubayeva played in the Tata Steel Challengers 2026, which ran until February 1. FIDE rating math means this tournament will be evaluated for the March 1 list. Facing an extremely strong field (avg rating ~2580), it is very difficult for a sub-2500 classical player to match their expected score without risk of a rating loss. Any rating drop without an offset from another event would push her strictly below 2497, ceding her ranking to Deshmukh (assuming Deshmukh is inactive or gains) and dropping Assaubayeva to 12th or worse (resolving NO). We also account for a minor (~10-15%) chance that Wikipedia editors will be late by a few days to update the March table, automatically retaining the Feb 1 table which gives a YES. Factoring in her chances of exceeding expectations vs bleeding rating points, plus the Wikipedia update lag, estimates place her chances of maintaining a rank ≤ 11 at 40%.
